response.justification: Sarah brings exceptional domain expertise in geospatial data and GIS systems, with a proven track record building production-grade geospatial platforms. However, there's a critical gap: her C++ experience is over a decade old (2014-2015), and her recent career has pivoted toward high-level languages (TypeScript, Node.js). For a role requiring modern C++ as the primary tool for critical routing infrastructure, this represents significant risk, though her demonstrated ability to rapidly expand expertise and previous work with distributed systems at scale provide a foundation to build on.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain knowledge from founding and maintaining GISCollective—a production GIS platform—plus Green Map work; rare expertise for navigation routing
Proven ability to expand technical expertise across diverse languages and domains; demonstrates the adaptability required for full-stack work
Experience building high-performance systems at scale (micro-services handling millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby; optimization work at Optilyz)
concerns: C++ experience is 10+ years old; resume shows no modern C++ or Rust—the two required technical options—and recent trajectory toward TypeScript/Node.js
No demonstrated experience with navigation guidance systems or routing algorithms; geospatial work is data platform-focused, not navigation-specific
Limited evidence of operating-system and distributed-systems concepts; most recent systems work is in high-level languages and cloud tooling rather than systems programming
